payload.text:
Tạo một app: Vai trò của bạn: Bạn là một đạo diễn phim hành động ảo và kỹ sư tạo prompt cho Veo 3. Nhiệm vụ của bạn là viết kịch bản chi tiết gồm 38 scenes (mỗi scene 8 giây, tổng cộng ~5 phút) để tạo thành một video liền mạch theo phong cách game BeamNG.Drive (kênh Jota Drive).
Thông tin đầu vào:
• Tiêu đề/Chủ đề: [ĐIỀN TÊN CHỦ ĐỀ CỦA BẠN VÀO ĐÂY]
• Phong cách cốt truyện: Hài hước đen tối, trớ trêu, tập trung vào sự cố cơ học (mechanical failure), những kế hoạch thất bại ("mission of our lives" failed), xe quá nhiệt ("Heat up here"), và các tình huống vật lý hỗn loạn (bánh xe rụng, va chạm cảnh sát).
Cấu trúc Video (3 Hồi):
1. Hồi 1 (Scene 1-10): Thiết lập & Khởi đầu. (Ví dụ: Mua phải xe "sắt vụn" được quảng cáo là "premium car", hoặc lập kế hoạch cướp hàng "Fast and furious style").
2. Hồi 2 (Scene 11-25): Sự cố leo thang. (Ví dụ: Động cơ quá nhiệt, khói mù mịt, lốp nổ, kế hoạch đổ bể, mất đồng đội).
3. Hồi 3 (Scene 26-38): Cao trào & Kết thúc. (Ví dụ: Rụng bánh xe lăn vào xe cảnh sát, xe lết về đích trong tình trạng tàn tạ nhưng nhân vật vẫn vui vẻ tận hưởng "dream vacation").
Yêu cầu Kỹ thuật:
1. Số lượng: Viết đủ 38 Scenes (đánh số 01-38).
2. Độ dài mỗi scene: 8 giây.
3. Tính liền mạch: Scene sau phải nối tiếp logic vật lý và bối cảnh của Scene trước (vị trí xe, mức độ hỏng hóc tăng dần).
4. Quy tắc ngắt quãng (QUAN TRỌNG): Bạn PHẢI DỪNG LẠI và chờ tôi xác nhận "OK" sau mỗi 5 Prompts. Không được viết một mạch hết 38 cái.
Cấu trúc Prompt cho Veo (Bắt buộc tuân thủ cho từng Scene): Mỗi Scene phải được viết dưới dạng Prompt tiếng Anh theo công thức sau:
SCENE [X]: Prompt: [Subject] + [Action/Physics] + [Environment/Context] + [Camera Style/Lighting] Sound/Voice: (Gợi ý âm thanh hoặc thoại ngắn kiểu "Heat up here", "Another customer fooled"...)
Giải thích các thành phần:
• [Subject]: Mô tả đối tượng chính (xe, nhân vật, bộ phận xe). Lưu ý: Xe phải giữ nguyên màu sắc và đặc điểm qua các scene.
• [Action/Physics]: Mô tả hành động vật lý cụ thể (nổ lốp, khói bốc lên, hệ thống treo rung lắc, va chạm mềm).
• [Environment/Context]: Địa hình, bối cảnh xung quanh.
• [Camera Style/Lighting]: Góc quay (POV, Chase camera, Cinematic), ánh sáng.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BẮT ĐẦU: Hãy viết 5 Scene đầu tiên cho chủ đề tôi đã cung cấp ở trên.

**Formulating App Concept**

I'm solidifying the app's core function: crafting a 38-scene screenplay. Each scene should be 8 seconds long, inspired by BeamNG.drive and Jota Drive's dark humor. The aim is to create a dynamic virtual director tool, a "Virtual Action Movie Director and Veo 3 Prompt Engineer," as requested.


**Refining Scene Generation**

I'm now focusing on the prompt structure: `SCENE [X]: Prompt: [Subject] + [Action/Physics] + [Environment/Context] + [Camera Style/Lighting] Sound/Voice: (Gợi ý...)`. My aim is gritty, automotive scenes with carbon fiber black, neon orange, metallic silver, and road grey aesthetics. I'm building features: an input for the "Project Title/Topic", a dashboard for scene tracking, AI integration (Gemini 3 Flash/Pro), a progress bar, and a "Generate Next 5" button. First, I need to get the user to input the topic.


**Defining App Architecture**

I've been mapping out the app's structure. I'm focusing on the component breakdown: `SceneCard`, `ProgressBar`, and `DirectorDashboard`. I'm using TypeScript to define the `Scene` interface and building boilerplate code, including `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and `types.ts`. I'm considering a `metadata.json` file. I'll need a service file to manage the Gemini integration. The core is the `App.tsx` file for main logic and layout.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS
React initialization
Global types and interfaces
Logic for interacting with Gemini API to generate scripts
Main application component

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: cho người dùng chọn số scene muốn tạo mặc định 38, nếu người dùng chọn các số khác thì tùy chỉnh nội dung lại cho phù hợp độ dài

**Implementing Scene Generation Count**

Okay, so I'm finally moving forward with adding the scene count selection feature to the app. The core logic is now adapting to the dynamic scene number. I'm focusing on making the default scene count 38, but allowing users to set a preferred value. This dynamic total is now the driver for all future operations.


**Integrating Total Scene Count**

I've got the ball rolling on implementing the UI element for the scene count. I'm adding an input field right on the initial form, and I'm updating the `ScriptState` type to handle the `totalScenes` variable. The `generateScenesBatch` service is being modified to respect this new total, and I'm updating App.tsx component logic to calculate progress based on that input.

entries.description: Add totalScenes to ScriptState interface
Update service to handle dynamic total scenes and adjust pacing in the prompt
Add input for total scenes and update application logic to respect dynamic totals

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: sau khi có kết quả, hiện nút download Json để download toàn bộ prompt xuống dạng flie .json

**Considering Download Functionality**

I'm now focusing on integrating a "Download JSON" button. My initial assessment centers on how to structure the generated prompts within a `.json` file for user download. I'll analyze the current code to determine the best place for this function and the required modifications.

entries.description: Add a Download JSON button to the script completion screen to export generated prompts.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: sau khi có kết quả prompt, hiện nút SEO như sau: title 55 characters, write a description, 3 hashtags, 5 keywords separated by commas, text prompt for thumbnail, write in English (thumbnail prompt dưới 500 characters, chọn cảnh cao trào nhất làm thumbnail, chữ tiếng anh ngắn nổi bật, to rõ in đậm, dễ viral)

**Analyzing Feature Implementation**

I'm currently focused on the technical implementation of the SEO feature for the Veo 3 Action Director. I've identified two key updates: updating `types.ts` with a new `SeoData` interface, and modifying `services/` to integrate this new SEO functionality after script generation. Next, I'll flesh out how this data will be surfaced within the user interface.


**Developing SEO Assets**

I've been working on integrating SEO functionality, specifically focusing on generating SEO content. I've added a function to `geminiService.ts` to create SEO assets based on the script. `App.tsx` will now handle the display. A "Generate SEO Assets" button will trigger this analysis after script generation. Clicking this will call Gemini to create the title, description, hashtags, keywords and a thumbnail prompt. I'm aiming for a copyable output format.

entries.description: Add SeoData interface and update ScriptState
Add generateSeoAssets function to the gemini service
Integrate SEO generation logic and UI

payload.text: Cập nhật thêm: sau khi có thông tin SEO thì hiện nút Copy All để copy tấc cả thông tin SEO vào clipboard, chỗ thumbnail: hiện nút Tạo ảnh: cho người dùng chọn Text Prompt phù hợp, Model tạo ảnh, Kiểu ảnh: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, nếu trong quá trình tạo ảnh thumbnail bị lỗi thì hiện nút tạo lại, lấy text thumbnail mà người dùng chọn đặt tên cho ảnh dạng không dấu.

**Expanding the Application Features**

I'm currently focused on the "Veo 3 Action Director" app. My primary concern right now is the inclusion of SEO and thumbnail generation. I have added a "Copy All SEO Info" button. The next step is adding a "Generate Image" feature for the thumbnails. I intend to make a text-based generation option for the thumbnails as well.


**Developing Thumbnail Generation**

I'm currently working on the thumbnail generation feature. I've defined the image generation inputs: a pre-filled, editable prompt, model and aspect ratio selections. I've also incorporated error handling with a "Retry" button. I plan to allow downloading with a filename derived from the prompt text. I have specified two models, `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`. Now I'm focused on integrating the UI elements and the backend calls. I have started on the file sanitization for download.


**Implementing Image Generation Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the image generation implementation. I've simplified the API key handling, defaulting to the environment variable except for Veo. I'm focusing on the file sanitization for downloading, removing accents, replacing spaces, and removing special characters. The state management in `App.tsx` is being refined to handle image generation, including error states and configuration, such as the model, aspect ratio, and prompt. I am also working on improving the UI.

entries.description: Update ScriptState to handle image generation and configuration.
Add image generation service function using nano banana models.
Implement SEO "Copy All", Thumbnail generation UI, and image download with sanitized filename.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm chỗ tạo ảnh thumbnail: khi tạo ảnh thumbnail thì chọn một trong các GỢI Ý CHỮ TRÊN THUMBNAIL để thêm text nổi bật lên thumbnail

**Improving Thumbnail Suggestions**

I'm currently focused on improving thumbnail generation for "Veo 3 Action Director". My priority now is creating a set of suggested texts for thumbnails to give the user more options. I'm aiming for flexibility and user control in this process, ensuring they can easily customize these suggestions to fit their video content.


**Integrating Text Suggestions**

I'm now integrating the selected text into image generation for "Veo 3 Action Director." I've updated the plan by incorporating thumbnail text suggestions into `SeoData` and modifying `generateSeoAssets` to create viral English text options. In the `App` component, I will add state for tracking selected text, display clickable suggestions, and ensure they are added to the prompt, which is crucial for thumbnail generation.


**Refining Text Selection**

I'm refining the text selection UI for "Veo 3 Action Director." The focus is now on giving users *explicit* buttons for picking thumbnail text from suggestions. I've updated `SeoData` to include `thumbnailTextSuggestions` and will modify the Gemini call to generate 3-5 options. In `App.tsx`, I'll introduce state for the selected text, display clickable buttons, and ensure the chosen text gets added to the image prompt during thumbnail generation.

entries.description: Add thumbnailTextSuggestions to SeoData interface.
Update generateSeoAssets to include thumbnail text suggestions.
Update App component to include thumbnail text selection and improved prompt handling.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: đề xuất thêm 5 chủ đề tiếp theo để người dùng chọn, khi người dùng chọn 1 chủ đề thì tiếp tục quy trình lại từ đầu bằng chủ đề người dùng vừa chọn

**Designing Recommended Themes**

I'm working on how to add a "Recommended Themes" feature. Currently, the plan involves suggesting five themes after a script completes. Clicking a suggestion will then restart the process with the new theme.


**Detailing Theme Selection**

I'm now diving into the implementation. I've updated `types.ts` to include `nextThemeSuggestions` in `SeoData`. I'm also modifying the `generateSeoAssets` function's prompt in `geminiService.ts` to include this new data. In `App.tsx`, I'll add a section for display and the `handleSelectTheme` function to reset the state and restart with the new theme. The `handleSelectTheme` logic will update `inputTitle`, reset `currentBatch`, and reset `scenes`.


**Refining Restart Logic**

I've decided to refine the restart mechanism after a theme selection. Instead of automatically restarting the process, I'll update the `inputTitle` based on the selected theme, allowing the user to review and adjust before starting production. I am implementing this change in response to the original prompt, which seemed to suggest automation. I'll need to modify the SEO generation prompt and update the UI accordingly. I also made a mental note to confirm image generation model names are consistent.


**Reviewing Content Generation Rules**

I am now reviewing the content generation rules, particularly for images. I confirmed that the correct `ai.models.generateContent` method is being utilized for the nano banana models, so that's good. I also verified that no specific permissions are needed according to the `metadata.json` file for this particular request. I've updated the plan to include a `handleResetAndStart` function, a refinement for displaying the suggestions within a "What's Next?" section post-SEO results, and a refactoring to handle the flow to the suggested theme using `setInputTitle`.

entries.description: Add nextThemeSuggestions to SeoData interface to store suggested topics for the user.
Update generateSeoAssets to include 5 next theme suggestions in the response.
Add Next Theme suggestions UI and click handler to restart production with the chosen topic.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm trong phần đề xuất chủ đề tiếp theo hiện nút copy all để copy hết các chủ đề vào clipboard

**Considering Theme Suggestions**

I'm thinking about adding a "Copy All" button to the "Recommended Next Themes" in `App.tsx`. The goal is to easily copy the current list of theme suggestions (which is an array of strings in `SeoData`) to the clipboard, simplifying the workflow.

entries.description: Add a "Copy All" button to the Recommended Next Themes section to copy all suggested topics to the clipboard.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: khi nhấn chạy thì chạy một lần đủ số prompt yêu cầu luôn khỏi hỏi lại

**Developing Automation Further**

I'm now focused on streamlining the app's production workflow. My aim is to eliminate the pause every five scenes, enabling the system to auto-generate all subsequent scenes when the "Start Production" button is clicked. This should significantly speed up the user's experience and overall production time. The goal is a truly seamless, hands-off automation process for scene generation.


**Refining Continuous Scene Generation**

I'm now implementing continuous scene generation within `App.tsx`, eliminating the manual batching. Instead of a single batch, I'm refactoring the `handleStart` function to initiate a loop. This loop will repeatedly call `generateScenesBatch` (which I'll keep at batch size 5 internally for efficiency), updating the state to reflect progress. The loop will continue until the total scene count is reached. I'll need to modify or replace `handleNextBatch` to facilitate this new, automated workflow.


**Automating Batch Initiation**

I'm now refactoring `handleStart` in `App.tsx` to loop continuously, generating scene batches automatically. The loop will run until the state reaches the `totalScenes`. Each loop iteration will fetch a batch of 5 scenes and append to the current state. The "Confirm OK & Continue" step is getting removed, as it's not needed for fully automated generation.


**Automating Scene Generation Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on fully automating the scene generation process. The user desires the removal of the "Confirm OK & Continue" step. I'm modifying `handleStart` in `App.tsx` to call a dedicated function that generates all batches sequentially using a loop. After each batch, the state is updated to show visual progress to the user. I've noted that `currentBatch` is the relevant state to be updated, and I must use functional updates within the loop to correctly manage React state changes. I'll need to refactor `handleStart` to initiate this new loop.


**Implementing Full Automation Loop**

I'm now integrating a complete automation loop within the `handleStart` function. I've switched from batch-by-batch calls to a loop that generates all the necessary batches sequentially. The state is updated within the loop after each batch. Also, I realized that I had to add the feature "tiếp tục quy trình lại từ đầu bằng chủ đề người dùng vừa chọn", and I will be putting it inside `startProduction`. I'm also now considering the effects of generation time, should a single linear process not be suitable.


**Verifying Progress Bar Updates**

I'm now making sure the progress bar updates accurately during the full automation loop. The "Confirm OK & Continue" button is now removed. I've integrated `startProduction` in `App.tsx` for iterative generation. The UI will update the current batch and total scenes. The user starts and the progress bar just fills up. I'm verifying that the loop, now using `await` inside, is safe against browser timeouts. `handleNextBatch` is removed and logic is now inside a loop in `startProduction`. I'm confident that the service will remain unchanged.


**Adjusting Start Production Logic**

I'm now changing `startProduction` to handle the iterative batch generation more effectively. The function will initialize state, calculate the number of batches needed, and then run a loop. Inside the loop, I'll fetch each batch using `generateScenesBatch` and append it to `allScenes`. The state is updated after each batch, showing the progress. This approach will replace the previous one-off call, providing a streamlined and efficient process.


**Streamlining UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on streamlining the UI during scene generation. The "Confirm OK & Continue" button will be removed entirely since automation is the goal, and it won't be displayed at all during automatic generation. Instead, the UI will reflect the "Full Script Ready" state when the process completes. I'm modifying the button's conditional display logic in the `App` component to remove it completely when generation starts.


**Eliminating Manual Confirmation**

I'm now completely removing the manual "Confirm OK" step from the scene generation flow, and the loop will be fully automatic. The challenge is handling potential errors within the automation. If an API call fails during the scene generation, I will need to consider how to handle a potential resumption, although the focus is still on a single uninterrupted run. I'll prioritize a clean and automated loop within `startProduction`, incorporating visual UI updates with each completed batch.

entries.description: Update startProduction to automatically loop through all batches of scenes until completion, removing the need for manual confirmation between batches.

payload.text: cập nhật thêm: sau khi viết xong tấc cả các prompt yêu cầu thì hiện nút SEO kế nút Export DPF

**Reviewing the Layout**

I'm currently reviewing `App.tsx` and it looks like the SEO button's conditional rendering is already situated where I need it, next to the PDF export. No major code refactoring should be necessary, which is great! I will next check conditional rendering for the SEO button and adjacent buttons.


**Analyzing Button Placement**

I've examined the button rendering logic. It seems the "Get SEO & Thumbnail" button already conditionally appears next to the "Export PDF" button when all scenes are written and SEO data is unavailable. The code functions as requested, by rendering the SEO button next to PDF export button when appropriate. I am now checking if this logic can be simplified further.


**Consolidating Button Placement**

I'm now focusing on how the "SEO" functionality integrates with the "Export PDF" action. I'm considering always displaying an "SEO" button adjacent to "Export PDF" when all scenes are ready, regardless of SEO data generation status. This aligns with the user's explicit request to have these actions grouped. I'll rename "Get SEO & Thumbnail" to "SEO" or "Generate SEO" to simplify its meaning. I will also assess whether a re-generation option is preferred.


**Implementing the SEO Button**

I'm now revising the code to make the "SEO" button always visible next to "Export PDF." Previously, the button only appeared when SEO data wasn't available, but now it'll be present regardless. The new approach uses a simplified condition to always display it, even if SEO data already exists, allowing direct access to SEO actions.


**Prioritizing Button Order**

I'm now changing the order of the buttons to "Download JSON," "SEO," and then "Export PDF." The "SEO" button will be placed directly before "Export PDF," as requested. The "SEO" button will always be visible, but I'm considering text changes based on the availability of SEO data. I've also confirmed that the existing scene count logic correctly handles the "after writing all prompts" condition for when the SEO button should become available.


**Revising Button Visibility**

I'm now refining the button section in `App.tsx`. Currently, the SEO button is conditionally rendered based on `!state.seoData`. However, the section with SEO assets is already shown above when `state.seoData` exists. This makes me reconsider if the button should disappear after SEO generation, as the prompt was "show SEO button next to Export PDF." It seems cleaner to always show it next to "Export PDF."


**Finalizing Button Placement**

I'm now implementing the final button arrangement. I'll make the "SEO" button always visible, and next to "Export PDF", as the previous instructions. It will be shown next to "Export PDF" after the scenes are generated. I'm also ensuring the visibility logic aligns with the instruction that the button should appear "after all prompts are done." The "SEO" button will get a style update too. I'll need to update `App.tsx` and verify the logic with `inputTotal`. I also am making the SEO button look nice.


**Evaluating Conditional Logic**

I'm now diving into the `App.tsx` file and examining the `useEffect` hook that updates the custom image prompt when `state.seoData` is available. I'm assessing how this integrates with the overall flow, specifically how it impacts the visibility and behavior of the new SEO button. This is to ensure the button behaves as intended.


**Updating Thumbnail Handling**

I'm now focusing on how the thumbnail text suggestions are handled. The existing code checks for and sets a default thumbnail text if suggestions are available in `state.seoData`. I'm also ensuring this update aligns with the overall button logic I've been working on, making sure the changes work together harmoniously.


**Refining Error Handling**

I'm now integrating error handling for SEO and thumbnail generation. I'm ensuring error states are properly managed and displayed, providing informative feedback to the user on failures. The user is able to see if the process fails.


**Implementing Clipboard Functionality**

I'm now integrating the clipboard functionality for the SEO data. I've added functions to copy the title, description, keywords, hashtags, thumbnail prompt, and suggested texts to the clipboard, providing the user with simple methods to copy these to clipboard. I'm also implementing the copy of the next themes suggestions to the clipboard.

**Adding Image Downloads**

I've just added a function to sanitize filenames for downloads, and implemented the `downloadImage` function, which allows users to download the thumbnail. The filename is now based on either the selected thumb text or just "thumbnail". This means that it is now able to download images with their given or default names.

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:

```
Gemini generation error:
{"error":{"code":429,"message":"You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, head to: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/rate-limits. To monitor your current usage, head to: https://ai.dev/rate-limit. ","status":"RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED"}}
```

**Tackling API Exhaustion**

I'm currently focused on the `429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` error. It seems I've hit some API rate limits or quota issues while trying to "fix the following errors". As a senior frontend engineer, I'm thinking of a more robust error-handling approach to deal with these specific issues.


**Implementing Retry Logic**

I've been working on integrating retry logic, especially for the `429` error. My current focus is on adding an `withRetry` helper function in `geminiService.ts`. This function will handle exponential backoff to avoid hammering the API. I'm also planning to enhance the UI in `App.tsx` to provide more informative error messages when a quota is exhausted. The goal is to give users guidance on how to resolve these issues.


**Refining Error Messaging**

I'm now focusing on refining the error messages displayed to the user after retries. Specifically, I'm thinking about including a clear explanation of quota limits and a link to Google's documentation or billing information when a 429 error persists. I plan to use a warning style in the UI to visually distinguish quota errors from other technical failures, improving user experience. I'll modify `services/geminiService.ts` to integrate this detailed messaging.


**Adjusting Retry Strategy**

I'm revisiting the retry strategy, specifically the error handling in `geminiService.ts`. I noticed the error response is a stringified object, which I need to parse to extract the error details. I'll modify the retry logic to check for the actual error code, like `429`, instead of relying solely on string matching. In `App.tsx`, I'll update the UI to display user-friendly messages for quota errors, considering the free tier limitations and suggesting retries.


**Focusing on Implementation Steps**

I'm now zeroing in on the coding specifics. I'm focusing on modifying `geminiService.ts` to include the `withRetry` helper for both `generateScenesBatch` and `generateSeoAssets`. I'm also ensuring error extraction preserves the status code. In `App.tsx`, I'll enhance the error display, handling "Quota exceeded" with a billing docs link for 429 errors. The retry will be managed within the service. I'm ready to write the code.

entries.description: Implement exponential backoff retry logic to handle 429 Quota Exceeded errors gracefully.
Improve error UI to specifically handle Quota Exceeded errors with a helpful link to documentation.
